name: I hurt my knee in a Boise warehouse twice last month and thought it would get better. Is it too late to file?

mainEntity.name: I hurt my knee in a Boise warehouse twice last month and thought it would get better. Is it too late to file?

acceptedAnswer.text: It\'s likely not too late, but you must act now. In Idaho, you have 60 days for notice. If you\'re still within that window, report it in writing immediately to preserve your rights.
acceptedAnswer.description:
In Idaho, you generally have 60 days to give notice of an injury to your employer, while Utah is 30 days. However, the formal "statute of limitations" to file an actual claim with the state commission is usually one to two years. If you miss the reporting deadline, your claim can be dismissed entirely, even if your injury is obvious.

The 11 pm "freak-out" is common—if you haven\'t reported it yet and the accident was a few days ago, do it first thing tomorrow morning via email or text so there is a time-stamped record.

Timing "Gray Areas":
- Noticing a "slow" injury like carpal tunnel or a worsening back.
- When an employer says "don\'t worry, we\'ll take care of it" but never files the papers.
- Deadlines for appealing a denial (which are often only 30 days).

Don\'t wait for the pain to "go away." If it doesn\'t, and you haven\'t reported it, you may be stuck with thousands of dollars in medical debt.

A lawyer ensures that all state-specific forms are filed with the correct government agencies before any deadlines expire.
